hard.next_step: Next step is for Zycus to share the slide deck and demo availability with Qais. Qais will then discuss internally with his Head of Department and work on arranging a broader demo with procurement, MLTC, and contracts stakeholders, potentially followed by IT/IDS, cybersecurity, budget alignment, and VP-level escalation if interest progresses.
hard.ais_why: Oman Oil (now operating under the OQ Group) is actively advancing digital transformation and operational excellence across its energy and procurement functions. As a large enterprise with complex supplier networks and capital-intensive operations, AI can deliver significant value in strategic sourcing, supplier risk management, contract lifecycle management, spend analytics, and autonomous procurement processes.

north_star_verdict.headline: A 19-day-old $100k qualified deal that has gone quiet before it ever got going: our only contact is evaluator-level Qais Al Ajmi, no economic buyer is named, and the 30 Jun opportunity-health review already flags the prospect as cooling with no buyer inbound in 14 days. The 24 Jun 2027 close gives a long recovery runway, but there is no verified buyer engagement to build on, so the immediate job is to convert the planned deck-and-demo next step into a real two-way conversation and multi-thread beyond Qais.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-17, moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 2026-06-30, so ~6 days in Qualified and ~19 days old as of the 06 Jul sweep. Close date 2027-06-24 is 353 days out. LastActivityDate is null and no Avoma call matched after opp+account+attendee discovery, so there is no logged buyer engagement. Submission dates (Formal_Eval, Shortlisted) are unpopulated, consistent with an early Qualified deal. Last modification 2026-07-02 was a Next_Step update (rep-side). Pace required: the buyer has not entered a demo or evaluation, so with a 12-15 month enterprise cycle the 2027 date is credible only if a real demo lands in the next 30 days.
evidence: No verified buyer engagement: LastActivityDate is null and no Avoma call matched after full opp+account+attendee discovery.
Prospect cooling: the 30 Jun opportunity-health tasks flag 'no inbound since last 14 days' and '10 days without email outbound'.
Single-threaded to Qais Al Ajmi (Section Lead Local Procurement & MLTC, evaluator role); no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker engaged.
Next step (share deck plus demo availability with Qais, updated 02 Jul) is a rep-side push awaiting a buyer reply, not buyer engagement.
Pipeline forecast is honest for an unengaged early qualified deal, so the category is defensible; the risk is engagement, not the number.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists for this deal. There is no Avoma call on record and no buyer-stated cost, cycle-time, compliance, or savings figure anywhere in the Salesforce facts. The only value context is the AIS rationale (17 Jun), which is a model-inferred read of OQ Group's digital-transformation appetite, not a buyer-articulated metric. Because the buyer has not yet been in a real discovery conversation, the pain is unquantified by definition. The move is to use the planned broader demo with procurement and MLTC leads to discover and quantify the cost of the current SAP-plus-Tareed process (manual benchmarking effort, tender cycle time, single-source spend leakage).

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is unmapped. The only two contact roles are Qais Al Ajmi (Section Lead Local Procurement & MLTC, Evaluator) and Khalid Al Mamari (Head of Project Services, Influencer), neither of whom carries budget authority. The Next_Step (02 Jul) names escalation to Qais's 'Head of Department', 'budget alignment', and 'VP-level escalation' as future steps, but no budget owner is named or engaged, and there is no call from which to infer one, so I will not assign the role to a person. The deal cannot credibly reach Shortlisted or pricing without an economic buyer, so the ranked move is to ask Qais to name the Head of Department and the VP who owns the procurement-transformation budget and secure an introduction.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's evaluation criteria are not yet documented from any two-way conversation. No RFP, RFI, use-case review, or workshop has occurred, and no Avoma call exists to surface stated criteria. The implied criteria from the deal shape (17 Jun) are strategic sourcing, supplier risk, contract lifecycle, and how far AI can automate complex single-source item and brand benchmarking, plus coexistence with the existing SAP and Tareed stack, but these are inferred from the product scope and process notes, not confirmed by the buyer. The move is to run structured discovery in the first real demo to capture OQ's ranked requirements before positioning.

decision_process.narrative: The intended decision process is described only as the rep's forward plan, not a buyer-confirmed map. Next_Step (02 Jul) lays out a sequence: Qais shares internally with his Head of Department, then a broader demo with procurement, MLTC, and contracts stakeholders, potentially followed by IT/IDS, cybersecurity, budget alignment, and VP-level escalation. This is a credible enterprise chain for OQ, but every stage past Qais is prospective, no meeting is booked, and no buyer has confirmed the sequence. Reading the (thin) activity trail, the process has not actually started moving on the buyer side. The move is to get the broader demo scheduled so the real process map (who joins, who approves) becomes visible.
sources: Field history StageName Initial Interest to Qualified (30 Jun)

paper_process.narrative: The paper process is unknown, which is normal at Qualified stage. No MSA, SOW, procurement channel, or contract-expiration forcing function is on record, and OQ's tendering runs through the Tareed portal, which itself hints procurement may route any purchase through a formal tender rather than direct paper. Next_Step (02 Jul) anticipates IT/IDS and cybersecurity review downstream, signalling an InfoSec gate will apply, but no timeline exists. The move is to ask, once the broader demo lands, whether OQ will buy via direct contract or a Tareed tender, and what legal and InfoSec approvals apply, so the paper path can be mapped before commercials.
sources: Prior record: SAP + Tareed process (17 Jun)

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer, because there has been no genuine discovery call. From the account context (17 Jun) the inferred pain is a manual, SAP-plus-Tareed procurement process across a complex, capital-intensive supplier network, with likely friction in single-source item and brand benchmarking and in tender management, but this is inferred from the stack and product scope, not confirmed, and no pain owner is named. The move is to run discovery in the broader demo to have procurement and MLTC leads state their own pain and name who owns it.
sources: Prior record: SAP + Tareed process (17 Jun)

champion.narrative: No champion is identified. Qais Al Ajmi (Section Lead Local Procurement & MLTC) is the only active contact but is evaluator-level and shows no advocacy evidence: no introductions to the Head of Department or a VP, no shared internal material, no competitive intel, and no agenda-driving, and the prospect has in fact gone quiet (no inbound in 14 days per the 30 Jun health review). Khalid Al Mamari (Head of Project Services) sits on the record as an Influencer but has never been engaged. The deal is single-threaded to a coach-at-best contact with no proven access to power. The move is to earn Qais as a coach by delivering a strong first demo, then test whether he will introduce his Head of Department and the budget owner.
sources: SF Task 'Prospect's gone cold? No inbound since last 14 days' (30 Jun)

competition.narrative: There is no named competing S2P vendor on record; the only incumbent is OQ's current process, SAP for requisition and RFQ creation with the Tareed portal for tendering (carried from the 17 Jun record). No Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, or Ivalua has surfaced, and it is unknown whether OQ is running a formal competitive RFP, so this is closer to unknown than sole-sourced. The wedge is to position Zycus as a modular, AI-led sourcing and supplier-management layer that integrates with SAP and Tareed rather than replacing them, leading with Merlin agentic AI for supplier benchmarking and negotiation automation. The move is to confirm during re-engagement whether other vendors are in the evaluation and whether a tender is planned.
sources: Prior record: SAP + Tareed process (17 Jun)

deal_movement.summary: All movement in the last 30 days is internal and administrative, not buyer engagement: an ownership reassignment, a stage bump to Qualified, and a batch of opportunity-health call-attempts, with the last real touchpoint being a rep-side Next_Step update on 02 Jul. The prospect side has been silent.

items.change: Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified
Owner reassigned to Mohamad Alhakim (RVP), co-owner updated
Eight internal opportunity-health call-attempts logged, flagging cold prospect and unmapped decision maker
Next_Step updated (share deck plus demo availability with Qais)

competitive_position.summary: No competing S2P vendor is named as of the 06 Jul sweep; the only incumbent is OQ's current process, SAP for PR and RFQ creation plus the Tareed portal for tendering (recorded 17 Jun). The real current question is not which rival is ahead but whether OQ will run a competitive RFP at all, which is still unknown. The strongest present force is therefore the status quo and integration inertia, not a competitor: OQ can keep running SAP-plus-Tareed and defer, so the wedge is a modular Merlin AI layer that augments rather than rips out that stack.

competitors.name: SAP + Tareed (incumbent process)
competitors.quote: OQEP uses SAP as the core procurement system for PR and RFQ creation; RFQs and tenders are then moved into the Tareed portal for tendering activities such as clarifications, drawings, offers, and supplier communication.
competitors.source: Prior canonical record (SF process fields, 17 Jun)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as a modular AI-led sourcing and supplier layer that integrates with SAP and Tareed rather than replacing them; make Merlin agentic AI for supplier benchmarking and negotiation the Phase-1 differentiator.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No buyer-stated expectations exist yet; the only fit signal is inferred from the deal shape. Positioning fit will be untestable until a real discovery conversation happens.

items.criterion: Coexistence with the existing SAP and Tareed stack
items.quote: Concerns around SAP and Tareed coexistence and integration complexity (carried from prior record).
items.source: Prior canonical record (SF Why_not_Zycus__c / Gaps_identified, 17 Jun)

items.deliverable: Qais to discuss internally with his Head of Department and arrange a broader demo with procurement, MLTC, and contracts stakeholders
items.grounding_quote: Qais will then discuss internally with his Head of Department and work on arranging a broader demo with procurement, MLTC, and contracts stakeholders.

No buyer-stated cost, cycle-time, compliance, or savings metric anywhere.

items.area: Evaluation structure unknown (RFP vs direct, other vendors)
items.quote: Unknown whether OQ runs a competitive tender or has other S2P vendors in play.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a dark, single-threaded early deal; the win strategy is to convert a rep-side next step into a real buyer conversation and widen access before positioning.
flags: Multi-thread now: Qais is evaluator-level and the sole contact, so map and reach the Head of Department and a VP budget owner before the broader demo.
Lead the first demo with the Merlin agentic-AI sourcing and supplier-benchmarking story layered on SAP plus Tareed, turning the integration concern into the differentiator (AIS 8.5, 17 Jun).
Qualify the evaluation early: confirm whether OQ is running a formal tender and which other S2P vendors are in, since the competitive field is currently unknown.

critical_signals.text: There has been no genuine two-way buyer conversation yet, and the prospect has gone quiet with no inbound in the 14 days before the 30 Jun review.
Khalid Al Mamari, Head of Project Services, is on the record as an influencer but has never been engaged, and the Head of Department and a VP budget owner exist only as future escalations.
OQ still runs SAP for requisitions and RFQs with the Tareed portal for tendering; no rival S2P vendor has surfaced and it is unknown whether a competitive tender is planned.
No price, budget, or ROI has been discussed; the 100k figure is an internal placeholder with no buyer-confirmed budget owner.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Qais Al Ajmi is the only active contact but is evaluator-level with no advocacy evidence (no introductions, no internal docs, no agenda-driving), and the prospect has gone quiet per the 30 Jun health review. Khalid Al Mamari is an unengaged influencer. The deal is single-threaded with no proven access to power.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI fit on paper (AIS 8.5, Strong) with iSource, iSupplier, Merlin, and Agentic AI in scope, but the AI story has not yet been put in front of any buyer, so it is under-positioned. The next demo must carry the Merlin agentic-AI narrative for supplier benchmarking and negotiation.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score is 8.5 with AIS_Status Strong (17 Jun), a strong model-inferred read of OQ Group's digital-transformation and procurement-automation appetite. No Avoma call exists to confirm or contradict buyer AI appetite, so the excitement is model-side and not yet validated by the buyer.

items.detail: No economic buyer or decision maker mapped; single-threaded to an evaluator-level contact with escalation to Head of Department and VP still only planned.
No buyer-driven timeline; the 24 Jun 2027 close is 353 days out and not anchored to any buyer milestone or demo date.
Buyer's SAP-plus-Tareed stack raises a coexistence and integration concern that is unanswered and could stall a modular-layer pitch.

items.detail: No budget owner confirmed and no ROI discussed; the 100k amount is unvalidated against any buyer-stated budget.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling before it started: the 30 Jun opportunity-health review explicitly flags no buyer inbound in 14 days and 10 days without outbound, and there is no buyer call on record.
items: No buyer inbound in the 14 days before 30 Jun (SF health task).
No Avoma call matched after full opp+account+attendee discovery.
Latest touch is a rep-side Next_Step update (02 Jul), not buyer engagement.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A brand-new (17 Jun) 100k qualified deal that has gone quiet before it got going: our only contact is evaluator-level Qais Al Ajmi, no economic buyer is named, and the 30 Jun health review already flags no buyer inbound in 14 days. Strong AI fit on paper (AIS 8.5) is the one asset, but nothing has been put in front of the buyer.

win_position.text: We are barely on the board: this is a 19-day-old qualified deal single-threaded to Qais Al Ajmi (Section Lead Local Procurement & MLTC, evaluator-level), with no economic buyer named and no buyer call on record (contact roles, 30 Jun).

The one asset is fit, not traction: AIS is 8.5 (Strong) and OQ's SAP-plus-Tareed stack is a clean target for a Merlin agentic-AI sourcing layer, though that story has not yet reached the buyer (SF AIS_Why, 17 Jun).
The prospect has gone quiet with no buyer inbound in the 14 days before the 30 Jun health review, so what little momentum exists is rep-push only.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is entirely rep-side: the only recent activity is eight internal opportunity-health call-attempts logged 30 Jun, none of which drew a buyer reply.
The next step (share deck and demo availability with Qais) is a Zycus push that has been awaiting a buyer response since the 02 Jul update, with no demo booked.

customer_commitment.text: There is no buyer commitment: no scheduled demo, no named decision date, and the escalation to the Head of Department and a VP is Zycus's plan, not something the buyer has agreed to (Next Step, 02 Jul).

deal_risk.text: Single-thread with no economic buyer: if Qais deprioritizes, the deal goes dark, and the 30 Jun review already flags the prospect as cooling.
The 353-day runway to a 24 Jun 2027 close masks the real problem, that there is no verified buyer engagement to build on yet.

champion_strength.evidence: No champion; sole contact is an evaluator-level Section Lead with no advocacy evidence (OpportunityContactRole, 30 Jun).

economic_buyer.evidence: EB unmapped; escalation to Head of Department and VP is planned only (Next_Step, 02 Jul).

engagement.evidence: No buyer call and no inbound in 14 days per the 30 Jun opportunity-health review.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer conversation on record; no stated preference for Zycus or any rival.

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings, or automation impact discussed; no buyer call and no quantified value fields.

scope_change.detail: Product scope unchanged since creation; field history shows only a None-to-None Products edit (01 Jul), no module reduction or amount cut.
